rationale: Humanities is the smallest level-1 (1% of papers) because most empirical humanities papers I rerouted to Social Sciences (linguistics, anthropology, STS). What remains here is the genuinely humanistic core: history (where archives and primary documents are the method), philosophy, archaeology (as material culture, not anthropology), classics, literature studies that engage methodologically. Ten subspecialties — small but representative. I made History of Science / Medicine its own card because it's a healthy publishing community with distinct journals.

subspecialties.name: Philosophy
subspecialties.tagline: Analytic, ethics, philosophy of mind, philosophy of science.
covers: analytic philosophy, ethics, philosophy of mind, philosophy of science, philosophy of language, political philosophy, metaphysics, epistemology
J Phil, Mind, Philos Sci, Phil Stud

subspecialties.name: History
subspecialties.tagline: Political, social, cultural, intellectual, global history.
covers: political history, social history, cultural history, intellectual history, global history, comparative history, environmental history
AHR, Past Present, J Mod Hist
explicitly_excludes: history of science → History of Science & Medicine

subspecialties.name: History of Science, Medicine & Technology
subspecialties.tagline: Pre-modern and modern history of science, medicine, technology.
covers: history of science, history of medicine, history of technology, history of mathematics, science in colonial contexts
Isis, Br J Hist Sci, Hist Sci, BJHS
explicitly_excludes: sociology of recent science → Social Sciences (STS)

subspecialties.name: Archaeology
subspecialties.tagline: Material culture, dating methods, archaeological science, heritage.
covers: material culture analysis, archaeological dating methods, archaeological science (radiocarbon, isotopes), heritage and conservation, landscape archaeology
J Archaeol Sci, Antiquity, Am Antiq

subspecialties.name: Classics & Ancient World
subspecialties.tagline: Greek, Latin, ancient near east, Mediterranean antiquity.
covers: Greek and Latin philology, ancient Mediterranean history (when not modern), ancient near east studies, classical archaeology, classical reception studies
CQ, JRS, JHS

subspecialties.name: Literary Studies
subspecialties.tagline: Modern literature, criticism, world literature, comparative literature.
covers: literary criticism, world literature, comparative literature, modern and contemporary literature, postcolonial literary studies
PMLA, NLH, MLQ

subspecialties.name: Religious Studies & Theology
subspecialties.tagline: Comparative religion, scripture studies, religious history.
covers: comparative religion, scripture studies, religious history, theology as academic field, sociology of religion-from-humanities-angle
JAAR, Hist Religions, J Theol Stud

subspecialties.name: Art History & Visual Culture
subspecialties.tagline: Art history periods, museum studies, visual culture analysis.
covers: art history periods, museum studies, visual culture analysis, history of architecture, material culture studies
Art Bull, J Visual Culture
explicitly_excludes: digital humanities computational → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Music & Performing Arts Studies
subspecialties.tagline: Musicology, music theory, theatre studies, dance studies.
covers: musicology, music theory, ethnomusicology, theatre studies, dance studies, film studies as humanities
JAMS, Music Anal, TJ

subspecialties.name: Digital Humanities
subspecialties.tagline: Computational text analysis, archive digitization, visualization for humanities.
covers: computational text analysis applied to humanities, digital editions, archive digitization, visualization for humanities, distant reading, digital prosopography
DSH, DH Quarterly

uncovered_concerns: Considered Area Studies (e.g. East Asian Studies) — split across History, Literary Studies, and Social Sciences. No separate card.
Considered Gender & Sexuality Studies as humanities — most empirical work folds under Social Sciences; theoretical/literary work folds under Literary Studies.
Considered Indigenous Studies — folds across Anthropology (Social Sciences), History, and Literary Studies.
